_comment: Synthetic R6b metaethical-objectivism DISCRIMINANT cohort (scoring.md §20.5). 40 participants across four channels (objectivism / protected / identity / card_sort), a SHARED cohort keyed r00..r39. objectivism_moral_i is drawn INDEPENDENT of the three 'how much morality matters' predictors — sacredness |P_i| (R2), internalization centrality (R1), value-importance (aspirational card-sort mean) — so R² ~ 0, the upper 95% CI clears the 0.50 ceiling, and R6b is SUPPORTED: metaethical objectivism is a DISTINCT construct, not reducible to how absolute/central/broad a person's values are (Goodwin & Darley 2008). Consumed ONLY by analyze.py --r6b-log; isolated from the sample-objectivism-log R6a/R6d cohort. Generated by /tmp/gen_r6b.py (self-checking, not committed).
